meta.title: GCP-ADP-1: Core Data Services and Governance

questions.stem: A team wants to store raw files cheaply and durably before loading them into analytics systems. Which Google Cloud service is the primary object store for this use case?

options.explanation: Cloud Storage is Google Cloud's managed object storage service for files, data lakes, and staging areas.
BI Engine accelerates BigQuery queries rather than storing raw objects.
Cloud SQL is a relational database service.
Pub/Sub Lite is for messaging, not primary object storage.

questions.stem: A dataset is rarely accessed and should be stored at the lowest cost class for long-term retention. Which Cloud Storage class is the best fit?

options.explanation: Archive is the lowest-cost Cloud Storage class for data that is accessed very rarely and kept for long-term retention.
Standard is intended for frequently accessed hot data.
Nearline is for infrequent access, but it is not the lowest-cost archival class.
Regional SSD is not a Cloud Storage storage class.

questions.stem: Which Google Cloud service is the primary serverless enterprise data warehouse for SQL analytics?

options.explanation: BigQuery is Google Cloud's serverless analytics warehouse for SQL queries at scale.
Cloud Run runs containers.
Cloud Build builds software.
GKE runs Kubernetes clusters.

questions.stem: A team wants to query files in Cloud Storage without first loading them into native BigQuery storage. Which BigQuery feature should they use?

options.explanation: BigQuery external tables let analysts query data stored outside native BigQuery storage, including Cloud Storage.
BI Engine accelerates queries but does not define storage access.
CMEK secures data but does not make external data queryable.
Scheduled queries automate SQL execution, not storage format.

questions.stem: Which SQL capabilities are native to BigQuery? (Select TWO)

options.text: Working with nested and repeated data using `UNNEST`
options.explanation: BigQuery supports analytic SQL features such as `UNNEST` for repeated fields and window functions.
BigQuery supports joins and far more than single-table queries.

options.text: Indexes must be created before any query can run
options.explanation: BigQuery does not require traditional indexes before querying.

questions.stem: A team needs a managed stream and batch processing service built on Apache Beam concepts. Which Google Cloud service is the best fit?

options.explanation: Dataflow is Google Cloud's managed service for Apache Beam pipelines across batch and streaming workloads.
CDN serves cached web content.
Secret Manager stores secrets.
Cloud DNS manages DNS zones.

questions.stem: Which Google service is commonly used to build and share dashboards directly from BigQuery and other connectors?

options.explanation: Looker Studio is Google's dashboarding and reporting tool and can connect directly to sources such as BigQuery.
Cloud Scheduler triggers jobs rather than building dashboards.
Artifact Registry stores packages and container images.
Datastream is for database replication and change data capture.

questions.stem: A company wants a unified governance layer for data lakes and warehouses, including zones and data quality tasks. Which service should it evaluate first?

options.explanation: Dataplex is the Google Cloud data governance and management service for organizing, securing, and monitoring distributed data.
Cloud Shell is a shell environment.
Memorystore is managed Redis or Memcached.
Cloud Tasks queues asynchronous work.

questions.stem: Which Google Cloud service helps discover, inspect, and de-identify sensitive information such as PII?

options.explanation: Sensitive Data Protection, formerly Cloud DLP, is the Google Cloud service for inspection, classification, and de-identification of sensitive data.
Cloud Armor is a WAF and DDoS protection service.
Binary Authorization controls container deployment policy.
Cloud Deploy manages release pipelines.

questions.stem: A developer needs a high-throughput API for writing records into BigQuery tables in near real time. Which interface is designed for this?

options.explanation: The BigQuery Storage Write API is built for efficient streaming writes into BigQuery tables.
The Cloud Storage XML API writes objects, not BigQuery rows.
Cloud Logging ingests logs.
Dataproc Serverless is for Spark workloads.

questions.stem: A user wants to avoid scanning an entire BigQuery table when filtering by event date. Which design choice helps most directly?

options.explanation: Partitioning lets BigQuery prune data based on the partition filter, reducing scanned bytes.

options.text: Store the table in Cloud Storage Archive
options.explanation: Archive storage is unrelated to BigQuery table pruning.
Pub/Sub is messaging.
Cloud Run jobs do not change table layout.

questions.stem: A table is frequently filtered by `customer_id` after date pruning. Which BigQuery optimization can further improve performance?

options.explanation: Clustering organizes storage for selective filtering on clustered columns after partition pruning.

options.text: Moving the table to Cloud SQL
options.explanation: Cloud SQL is a different database service.
CDN does not optimize warehouse scans.
IAM Conditions affect access, not physical layout.

questions.stem: Which statements about Cloud Storage classes are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Standard is for frequently accessed data
options.explanation: Standard and Nearline are different access-pattern classes for object storage with different cost characteristics.

options.text: Nearline is intended for infrequently accessed data
Archive is designed for hot analytics data
options.explanation: Archive is for very cold data.

options.text: Storage class changes require rewriting all application SQL
options.explanation: Storage class selection does not rewrite SQL queries.

questions.stem: A data engineer wants a fully managed service to replicate database changes continuously into Google Cloud. Which service should they consider first?

options.explanation: Datastream is Google's serverless change data capture and replication service.
Cloud Build builds software.
Filestore is managed NFS.
Interconnect provides network connectivity.

questions.stem: Which tasks are a strong fit for Cloud Scheduler? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Cloud Scheduler runs cron-like scheduled jobs, often by invoking HTTP targets or Pub/Sub.

options.text: Starting a recurring job on a cron-like schedule

options.explanation: It does not provide warehouse storage management.

options.text: Providing a dashboarding layer for analysts
options.explanation: It is not a BI tool.

questions.stem: Which access model controls whether a principal can read data in a BigQuery dataset?

options.explanation: BigQuery access is governed through IAM and BigQuery-specific permissions and roles.
Build triggers automate CI/CD.
Themes only affect presentation.
Bigtable app profiles are unrelated.

questions.stem: An analyst wants to email a recurring dashboard snapshot to stakeholders from a reporting tool. Which product best matches that workflow?

options.explanation: Looker Studio is the reporting product used for dashboards and scheduled sharing workflows.
Secret Manager stores secrets.
Dataproc runs Spark/Hadoop.
Cloud Trace is for distributed tracing.

questions.stem: A team wants to load CSV files from Cloud Storage into a BigQuery native table on a recurring basis. Which approach is directly supported?

options.explanation: BigQuery supports load jobs from Cloud Storage into native tables for batch ingestion.
Spreadsheet import is not the only method and is not the standard at scale.
Cloud Armor is security middleware.
DaemonSets are a Kubernetes feature.

questions.stem: Which features help analyze data in BigQuery? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: BigQuery supports SQL objects and constructs such as views and window functions for analytics.
Flow logs are network telemetry.
Cloud NAT rules are networking configuration.

questions.stem: Which goals align well with Dataplex? (Select TWO)

options.text: Organizing data into lakes and zones
options.explanation: Dataplex organizes distributed data estates and provides governance and quality capabilities.

options.text: Running governance and data quality tasks

options.explanation: It does not replace the warehouse query engine.
Container images belong in Artifact Registry.

questions.stem: Which storage class is typically best for frequently accessed active datasets kept in Cloud Storage?

options.explanation: The **Standard** storage class is designed for data that is accessed frequently or serves active workloads.
Nearline is intended for data accessed less than once per month.
Coldline targets even less frequent access patterns.
Archive is for the rarest access patterns and the longest retrieval expectations.

questions.stem: Which service is best suited for running managed Spark and Hadoop workloads when a team still wants that ecosystem?

options.explanation: Dataproc is Google Cloud's managed Spark and Hadoop service.
Looker Studio is BI.
Cloud Functions runs event-driven code.
Bigtable is a NoSQL database.

questions.stem: Why would a team use a BigQuery view instead of copying data into a second table?

options.text: To reuse query logic without duplicating data
options.explanation: Views let teams encapsulate SQL logic while continuing to query the underlying data.
Durability is unrelated.
Views do not create network boundaries.
Pub/Sub ordering is unrelated.

questions.stem: A data steward wants to inspect text for possible credit card numbers and mask them before release. Which service should they use?

options.explanation: Sensitive Data Protection provides built-in detectors and de-identification methods for sensitive content.
Cloud Build is CI/CD.
Cloud Monitoring tracks metrics.
BI Engine accelerates analytics.

questions.stem: Which statements about Looker Studio are true? (Select TWO)

options.text: It can connect to BigQuery data
options.explanation: Looker Studio is the visualization and reporting layer and can use BigQuery as a source.

options.text: It is used for dashboards and reports
It is the primary service for training custom models
options.explanation: Model training is not its purpose.
IAM policy management happens elsewhere.

questions.stem: Which BigQuery feature allows analysts to query nested repeated arrays in a table?

options.explanation: `UNNEST` expands arrays so GoogleSQL can work with repeated fields.
`kubectl` is for Kubernetes.
`gsutil rsync` copies objects.
Armor rules are security controls.

questions.stem: A team must process data in both batch and streaming modes with one programming model. Which choices align with that requirement? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Dataflow is the managed runtime for Apache Beam, which supports both batch and streaming pipelines.
Looker Studio is for dashboards, not pipeline execution.
Buckets store data but do not provide a unified programming model.

questions.stem: A query scans far more bytes than expected because it filters on an unpartitioned timestamp column. Which redesign is likely to help most?

options.text: Partition the table on the relevant timestamp or date column
options.explanation: Partitioning on the filtering column enables partition pruning and usually reduces scanned data.

options.text: Move the data to Cloud Storage Archive
options.explanation: Archive storage is unrelated.

options.text: Switch the report to Looker Studio
options.explanation: A BI tool does not change warehouse layout.

options.text: Store the data in Secret Manager
options.explanation: Secret Manager is not data storage for analytics.

questions.stem: Which are examples of managing data on Google Cloud? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Data management includes access control and governance practices such as quality checks and stewardship.

options.text: Applying data quality and governance policies with Dataplex

options.explanation: DNS records do not replace analytics languages.

options.text: Using load balancer health checks as metadata
options.explanation: Health checks are not metadata governance.

questions.stem: An analyst wants a no-code dashboard over BigQuery results. Which service is the most direct option?

options.explanation: Looker Studio is designed for report and dashboard authoring over sources including BigQuery.
GKE Autopilot runs containers.
KMS manages encryption keys.
Spanner is a database service.

questions.stem: Which services are commonly involved in ingesting data into BigQuery? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Staged files in Cloud Storage and native BigQuery ingestion interfaces are common ingestion building blocks.
Private DNS zones are networking resources.
Binary Authorization governs software deployment.

questions.stem: Which service is designed to trigger jobs based on a cron schedule?

options.explanation: Cloud Scheduler is Google Cloud's managed cron service.
BI Engine accelerates analytics.
Cloud Armor is a security service.
CA Service manages PKI.

questions.stem: A team wants to standardize a frequent SQL transformation without materializing another full table. Which object is usually appropriate in BigQuery?

options.explanation: A view stores query logic and can be reused without duplicating the dataset.
VPC networks manage connectivity.
Cloud NAT handles egress.
Pub/Sub schemas validate messages.

questions.stem: A steward needs a central place to organize distributed data assets into lakes and zones while applying governance. Which managed capability best matches?

options.explanation: Dataplex is specifically designed to organize and govern distributed data estates using concepts like lakes and zones.
Build triggers automate CI/CD.
Firebase Hosting serves web apps.
AlloyDB Omni is unrelated.

questions.stem: Which tasks can Looker Studio help with? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Looker Studio focuses on visualization, dashboard authoring, and report sharing.
Node pools are Kubernetes administration.
VPC Service Controls are security perimeter configuration.

questions.stem: Which SQL dialect is the default and recommended option for querying BigQuery tables?

options.explanation: **GoogleSQL** is BigQuery's default and recommended SQL dialect for querying tables.
Legacy SQL still exists for older workloads, but it is not the default or recommended choice for new work.
YAML is a configuration format, not a BigQuery query dialect.
CSS is a styling language, not a SQL dialect.

questions.stem: Which service would you choose for managed Apache Spark when Dataflow is not the chosen runtime and Spark compatibility is required?

options.explanation: Dataproc is the Google Cloud managed service for Spark and Hadoop workloads.
Looker Studio is BI.
Cloud Armor is WAF/DDoS protection.
IAM Recommender suggests permission optimizations.

questions.stem: Why might a team use BigQuery external tables instead of loading every file immediately?

options.text: To query supported external data where it already resides
options.explanation: External tables can be useful when data should stay in external storage but still be queryable from BigQuery.
IAM still applies.

options.text: To guarantee lower cost in every case
options.explanation: Cost depends on the workload.
External tables do not replace the storage service.

questions.stem: Which actions support secure and governed analytics on Google Cloud? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Secure analytics depends on least-privilege access and appropriate handling of sensitive information.

options.text: Inspect and de-identify sensitive data when needed
Grant dataset editor to every user by default
options.explanation: Broad editor access violates least privilege.

options.text: Publish all raw buckets publicly for convenience
options.explanation: Public raw buckets create clear data-exposure risk.

questions.stem: Which BigQuery design choices can help reduce query cost or scanned bytes? (Select TWO)

options.text: Partition tables on common filter columns
options.explanation: Partitioning and clustering are direct BigQuery table design choices that can reduce scanned data for common access patterns.

options.text: Cluster tables on frequently filtered columns

options.explanation: Cloud DNS does not store dashboards.

options.text: Use Archive class for every Cloud Storage object regardless of use
options.explanation: Cloud Storage class selection does not optimize BigQuery native table scans.
